title: Xtra Velvety Ethiopian Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and earthy Ethiopian lentil stew with a velvety texture, featuring cornstarch and al-dente finish, perfect for a crowd, this macrobiotic-friendly dish is sure to please. It is made with red lentils and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: In a large pot, saute the onion, garlic, and ginger in a little water until the onion is translucent.
Add the lentils, vegetable broth, berbere spice, and salt to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the cornstarch to thicken the stew.
Continue to simmer for another 10 minutes, or until the lentils are al-dente.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Let the stew cool, then refrigerate or freeze for later use.
Reheat the stew gently, whisking constantly, until warmed through.
